directive.title: Fix residual audit findings from AUTO-058

auditor.summary: The build addresses all 6 residual audit findings at the code level, with correct implementations for pinch-zoom scale calculation, long-press hit-testing, cloneElement elimination, keyboard accessibility, and swipe velocity guard. However, the build fails on 5 of 8 acceptance criteria due to critical issues in test implementation and missing verification artifacts. The long-press handler has a stale closure bug that captures coordinates at pointerdown time instead of at timeout fire time. The test mocks are insufficient and would produce unreliable results. Two acceptance criteria (AC-7 regression testing and AC-8 performance benchmarking) are completely unaddressed. The build requires significant fixes to tests and the long-press timing bug before it can be accepted.

top_issues.description: The long-press timeout captures `event` from the closure of handlePointerDown, but by the time the timeout fires (500ms later), the pointer may have moved. The spec requires using containerRef.getBoundingClientRect() to convert clientX/clientY to local coordinates at the time of the long-press trigger, but the code captures event.clientX/clientY at pointerdown time, not at timeout fire time. This means the long-press coordinates are stale and inaccurate.

The Date.now mock setup is insufficient. The implementation calls Date.now() multiple times (in handlePointerDown and handlePointerUp), but the test only mocks two return values. This will cause the timing calculations to use undefined/NaN values, making the swipe velocity tests unreliable.

The test for cloneElement elimination uses a placeholder `expect(true).toBe(true)` instead of actually reading the file and checking for cloneElement occurrences. The test for XAxis domain attribute checks for an HTML attribute, but the mock renders a div with spread props, so the domain prop would be a React prop, not an HTML attribute.
